text:
```sh cloud/scripts/update-translations.sh
#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

# export PYTHONPATH="."
export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE="cloud.settings"

echo "Copying dev-template.env as .env"
cp dev-template.env .env

pybabel extract \
    -c NOTE \
    -c Translators \
    -s \
    -F pybabel.cfg \
    --no-wrap \
    --sort-output \
    --copyright-holder="Otovo AS" \
    --msgid-bugs-address="klette+i18n@otovo.com" \
    --project="Otovo Cloud" \
    --version="1.0" \
    --omit-header \
    --keywords=gettext_lazy \
    -o locale/django_temp.pot \
    cloud

# We create our own custom header with the language set, even
# of its just a template file. This is for enabling PhraseApp to
# use the key values as the english version automatically

now=$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')
cat > locale/django.pot <<EOF
msgid ""
msgstr ""
"Project-Id-Version: Otovo Cloud\n"
"POT-Creation-Date: ${now}\n"
"PO-Revision-Date: ${now}\n"
"Language-Team: i18n@otovo.com\n"
"MIME-Version: 1.0\n"
"Content-Type: text/plain; charset=UTF-8\n"
"Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit\n"
"Plural-Forms: nplurals=2; plural=(n != 1);\n"
"Language: en\n"
"X-Poedit-SourceCharset: UTF-8\n"
EOF

cat locale/django_temp.pot >> locale/django.pot
rm locale/django_temp.pot


pybabel update --ignore-obsolete --no-wrap --no-fuzzy-matching -i locale/django.pot -d locale -D django
```

Whats the default ENVIRONMENT variable when running in GH actions?
When running in GitHub Actions, the default environment variable `ENVIRONMENT` is not set by default. However, GitHub Actions provides a set of predefined environment variables that are set automatically, which you can use within your workflows. Some of the common environment variables include:

- `GITHUB_WORKFLOW`: The name of the workflow.
- `GITHUB_RUN_ID`: A unique number for each run within a repository.
- `GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER`: A unique number for each run of a particular workflow within a repository.
- `GITHUB_ACTION`: The name of the action.
- `GITHUB_ACTIONS`: Always set to `true` when GitHub Actions is running the workflow.
- `GITHUB_ACTOR`: The name of the person or app that initiated the workflow.
- `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`: The owner and repository name.
- `GITHUB_EVENT_NAME`: The name of the webhook event that triggered the workflow.
- `GITHUB_EVENT_PATH`: The path to the file containing the complete webhook event payload.
- `GITHUB_WORKSPACE`: The GitHub workspace directory path.
- `GITHUB_SHA`: The commit SHA that triggered the workflow.
- `GITHUB_REF`: The branch or tag ref that triggered the workflow.

If you need to use a specific `ENVIRONMENT` variable in your GitHub Actions workflow, you can set it manually by defining it in your workflow YAML file. Here's an example of how to set it:

```yaml
name: CI

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    env:
      ENVIRONMENT: production

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2
        with:
          python-version: '3.x'

      - name: Install Dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run update-translations script
        run: |
          bash cloud/scripts/update-translations.sh
```

In this example, the `ENVIRONMENT` variable is set to `production` and will be available to the script running in the `Run update-translations script` step. Adjust the value as needed based on your environment.
```yml cloud/.github/workflows/translations_update.yml
name: Update and push translation files from Phrase

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

permissions: write-all

env:
  # See ./github/README.md to learn how we use variables
  PYTHON_VERSION: ${{ vars.PYTHON_VERSION }}
  POETRY_VERSION: ${{ vars.POETRY_VERSION }}

  POETRY_CACHE_KEY: ${{ vars.POETRY_CACHE_KEY }}
  VENV_CACHE_KEY: ${{ vars.VENV_CACHE_KEY }}


jobs:
  phrase:
    if: ${{ github.event.issue.pull_request && github.event.comment.body == '@translate/cloud' }}
    runs-on: small-runner
    env:
      GITHUB_ACTIONS: true
    steps:
      - uses: shanegenschaw/pull-request-comment-trigger@v2.1.0
        id: check
        with:
          trigger: '@translate/cloud'
          reaction: rocket
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: '${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}'
      - uses: xt0rted/pull-request-comment-branch@v2
        id: comment-branch

      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        if: success()
        with:
          ref: ${{ steps.comment-branch.outputs.head_ref }}

      - run: git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
      - run: git rev-parse --verify HEAD
      - uses: ./.github/actions/install
      - name: Get phrase CLI
        run: |
          wget https://github.com/phrase/phrase-cli/releases/download/2.8.0/phrase_linux_arm64
          chmod +x ./phrase_linux_arm64
          mv ./phrase_linux_arm64 ./phrase
      - name: Set up .phraseapp.yml for config and auth
        run: |
          cp dev-template-phraseapp.yml .phraseapp.yml
          sed -i 's/<YOUR ACCESS TOKEN>/${{ secrets.PHRASEAPP_API_KEY }}/' .phraseapp.yml
      - run: ./scripts/update-translations.sh
        if: steps.check.outputs.triggered == 'true'
      - run: git config --global --add safe.directory /__w/cloud/cloud
      - uses: EndBug/add-and-commit@v9
        with:
          message: 'i18n: Update pot file with new strings'
          add: 'locale/django.pot'
          new_branch: ${{ steps.comment-branch.outputs.head_ref }}
        if: steps.check.outputs.triggered == 'true'
      - run: ./phrase push
        if: steps.check.outputs.triggered == 'true'
```


So I have these two files, translations_update and update-translations. I want to only run `cp dev-template.env .env` in the latter file when running in the GH action and never locally. Please suggest changes.
You can modify the `cloud/scripts/update-translations.sh` script to conditionally run the `cp dev-template.env .env` command only when running under GitHub Actions. One way to achieve this is to check for the `GITHUB_ACTIONS` environment variable, which is automatically set to `true` when the script is running in a GitHub Actions environment.

Here are the proposed changes to the `update-translations.sh` script:

### Modified `update-translations.sh`

```sh
#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

# export PYTHONPATH="."
export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE="cloud.settings"

if [ "${GITHUB_ACTIONS:-false}" == "true" ]; then
    echo "Running in GitHub Actions"
    echo "Copying dev-template.env as .env"
    cp dev-template.env .env
fi

pybabel extract \
    -c NOTE \
    -c Translators \
    -s \
    -F pybabel.cfg \
    --no-wrap \
    --sort-output \
    --copyright-holder="Otovo AS" \
    --msgid-bugs-address="klette+i18n@otovo.com" \
    --project="Otovo Cloud" \
    --version="1.0" \
    --omit-header \
    --keywords=gettext_lazy \
    -o locale/django_temp.pot \
    cloud

# We create our own custom header with the language set, even
# if its just a template file. This is for enabling PhraseApp to
# use the key values as the english version automatically

now=$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')
cat > locale/django.pot <<EOF
msgid ""
msgstr ""
"Project-Id-Version: Otovo Cloud\n"
"POT-Creation-Date: ${now}\n"
"PO-Revision-Date: ${now}\n"
"Language-Team: i18n@otovo.com\n"
"MIME-Version: 1.0\n"
"Content-Type: text/plain; charset=UTF-8\n"
"Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit\n"
"Plural-Forms: nplurals=2; plural=(n != 1);\n"
"Language: en\n"
"X-Poedit-SourceCharset: UTF-8\n"
EOF

cat locale/django_temp.pot >> locale/django.pot
rm locale/django_temp.pot


pybabel update --ignore-obsolete --no-wrap --no-fuzzy-matching -i locale/django.pot -d locale -D django
```

### Explanation

1. The script checks if the `GITHUB_ACTIONS` environment variable is set to `true`.
2. If it is, it outputs a message and runs the `cp dev-template.env .env` command.
3. If not, it skips this step.

In your GitHub Actions workflow (`translations_update.yml`), the `GITHUB_ACTIONS` environment variable is automatically set by GitHub, so no changes are needed there.

This approach ensures that the `cp dev-template.env .env` command is only executed when the script runs in a GitHub Actions environment, and not when run locally or in other environments.
Thanks! Can you also add a comment/docstring at the top of the GH action to describe what it does? I also want to copy this description to Notion for documentation purposes
Sure! Here is an updated version of your GitHub Actions workflow with a comment/docstring at the top describing what it does:

### Updated `translations_update.yml`

```yml
# This GitHub Actions workflow is responsible for updating and pushing
# translation files from Phrase when triggered manually via the GitHub 
# UI or by a comment on a pull request. It performs the following tasks:
# - Checks out the appropriate branch.
# - Installs necessary dependencies.
# - Downloads and configures the Phrase CLI.
# - Runs a script to update translation files.
# - Commits and pushes the updated translation files back to the repository.
# - Pushes the updated files to Phrase.

name: Update and push translation files from Phrase

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

permissions: write-all

env:
  # See ./github/README.md to learn how we use variables
  PYTHON_VERSION: ${{ vars.PYTHON_VERSION }}
  POETRY_VERSION: ${{ vars.POETRY_VERSION }}

  POETRY_CACHE_KEY: ${{ vars.POETRY_CACHE_KEY }}
  VENV_CACHE_KEY: ${{ vars.VENV_CACHE_KEY }}


jobs:
  phrase:
    if: ${{ github.event.issue.pull_request && github.event.comment.body == '@translate/cloud' }}
    runs-on: small-runner
    env:
      GITHUB_ACTIONS: true
    steps:
      - uses: shanegenschaw/pull-request-comment-trigger@v2.1.0
        id: check
        with:
          trigger: '@translate/cloud'
          reaction: rocket
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: '${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}'
      - uses: xt0rted/pull-request-comment-branch@v2
        id: comment-branch

      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        if: success()
        with:
          ref: ${{ steps.comment-branch.outputs.head_ref }}

      - run: git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
      - run: git rev-parse --verify HEAD
      - uses: ./.github/actions/install
      - name: Get phrase CLI
        run: |
          wget https://github.com/phrase/phrase-cli/releases/download/2.8.0/phrase_linux_arm64
          chmod +x ./phrase_linux_arm64
          mv ./phrase_linux_arm64 ./phrase
      - name: Set up .phraseapp.yml for config and auth
        run: |
          cp dev-template-phraseapp.yml .phraseapp.yml
          sed -i 's/<YOUR ACCESS TOKEN>/${{ secrets.PHRASEAPP_API_KEY }}/' .phraseapp.yml
      - run: ./scripts/update-translations.sh
        if: steps.check.outputs.triggered == 'true'
      - run: git config --global --add safe.directory /__w/cloud/cloud
      - uses: EndBug/add-and-commit@v9
        with:
          message: 'i18n: Update pot file with new strings'
          add: 'locale/django.pot'
          new_branch: ${{ steps.comment-branch.outputs.head_ref }}
        if: steps.check.outputs.triggered == 'true'
      - run: ./phrase push
        if: steps.check.outputs.triggered == 'true'
```

### Description to Copy to Notion

**GitHub Actions Workflow: Update and Push Translation Files from Phrase**

This GitHub Actions workflow is responsible for updating and pushing translation files from Phrase when triggered manually via the GitHub UI or by a comment on a pull request. It performs the following tasks:
1. Checks out the appropriate branch.
2. Installs necessary dependencies.
3. Downloads and configures the Phrase CLI.
4. Runs a script to update translation files.
5. Commits and pushes the updated translation files back to the repository.
6. Pushes the updated files to Phrase.

This workflow ensures that translation files are kept up to date and synchronized with Phrase, facilitating smooth localization and internationalization processes.

You can copy the above description to your Notion documentation for reference.
summary: Running Django translation update script in GitHub Actions
